message: So I'm making grilled cheese at like 10 p.m. last week—exhausted, not paying attention—and suddenly the smoke detector goes off. Not a gentle beep. A full *screaming* alarm that made me jump three feet. My first thought? 'Thank goodness that thing actually works.' I'd replaced the batteries six months ago on a lazy Sunday, and honestly forgot about it until that moment. Turned out the pan wasn't actually on fire, just way too hot. Crisis averted. But it hit me: that small habit—the one I'd almost skipped—was exactly what mattered. The little wins add up. A working alarm. An escape plan written down. A family chat about where to meet outside. None of it's dramatic, but together they're the difference between 'we handled it' and wishing we had. What's one small fire-safety thing you've done lately that made you feel a bit more solid?
